title: Marker-rich main region is node/entry fabric, not flat file table
evidence: 2557 marker spans in main region vs only 256 path-like strings; 2078 spans carry no clear string; mean span length about 50.6 bytes
implication: Need parser over node/tag spans, not naive "path + offset + size" scan

title: First clean path record is self/root-referential to 0x1760
evidence: Prelude before first clean path at 0x1782 contains little-endian 0x1760, the same as header root/main offset candidate
implication: 0x1760 likely anchors main node region and may appear in root-linked records

title: l/n/o markers behave like node/record classes rather than file extensions alone
evidence: Markers precede GXI/PSM/XML/PSS records with mixed but structured distributions; same extension appears under multiple tags
implication: Tag is probably structural class or storage mode, not simply resource type

title: Clear path strings carry local metadata but not direct absolute TNG offsets
evidence: FMV records show stable local u32/u16 neighborhoods and near-anchor numeric hints, but no exact raw absolute offsets adjacent to strings
implication: True offset/size likely lives in auxiliary node or numeric tables

title: Auxiliary numeric tables exist outside the string-bearing record slices
evidence: Aligned near-anchor numeric clusters appear in low-offset control regions (e.g. 0x180, 0x328, 0xDEC) as well as later areas
implication: Stage 59 should target low-offset numeric clusters and try linking them to root/node records
